notes: Smallest round-trip for kiro-cli: one user prompt, one text-only assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE TUI driver (kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools): headless --no-interactive writes NO session file and is invisible to the daemon (FINDINGS.md §7), and the REPL must outlive the observation window so the working→ready settle is captured. BACKFLOW (recording 2026-06-05-01-49-31): the live capture refuted the provisional two-session (proc-<PID> presession → UUID reconcile) birth model the assessment assumed. kiro writes its <uuid>.jsonl lazily, but when it appears the daemon mints the UUID session DIRECTLY in ready (transcript_new → state ready "new session created" → pid_discovered) — there is NO separate proc-<PID> pre-session row. So this is a single-session birth like claudecode (session_birth relative to start), not the codex presession→new_session shape. The state arc (ready→working→ready, never waiting) is fully observed on the one UUID session; pillar verdict (agent=yes, daemon=full) is unchanged. The scenario's TotalTokens > 0 criterion is NOT expressible/observable for kiro-cli — the JSONL carries no token/cost/model fields (sidecar holds credits + model_id; the parser does not read it), so the observations vector asserts tokens_nonzero=false, cost_nonzero=false, model unknown.

text: UUID session born directly in ready when the lazy transcript appears (single-session birth, no separate proc-<PID> presession)
PID bound to the UUID session
User prompt → working on the UUID session
Text-only assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
